hero.sub: An open-source platform to explore, analyze and query Spanish law through knowledge graphs, AI chat and interactive dashboards. Built on top of the legalize-es corpus.

stats.label: Integrated layers — API, graph, chat & dashboards
LLM providers supported via MCP tools
Spanish laws from the legalize-es corpus
Open source under the Apache 2.0 license

layersTitle: One platform. The whole legal stack.
layersSub: Every layer ships on the same **FastAPI core** and lives behind a single **typed contract**, so the React app and any external client share the **exact same data**.

layers.title: A typed contract over every Spanish law.
layers.body: FastAPI endpoints for laws, articles, versions, diffs, full-text search and stats — generated OpenAPI consumed by a strictly-typed React client.

layers.title: Cross-references as a navigable network.
layers.body: A NetworkX graph linking laws, articles and amendments. Walk neighbours, find shortest paths, surface the most central norms by PageRank.
bullets: Filterable by topic, status, period or relation type
Ready to swap NetworkX for Neo4j at scale

layers.title: A chatbot that actually reads the law.
layers.body: Bring your own model — Ollama, LM Studio, OpenAI, Anthropic or Google. A FastMCP server exposes the API as tools, so answers come from real data, not memorized text.
bullets: MCP tools: search law, fetch article, view diff, query graph
Streamed responses with inline source citations
Local-first by default — Ollama runs offline

layers.title: Compliance and analytics at a glance.
layers.body: Plotly dashboards for legislative analysis and compliance tracking — reform velocity by year, most-amended laws, sector-level alerts.
bullets: Compliance panel · sector-scoped norms and alerts
Analytics panel · reform velocity, amendments, trends
Plotly figures served as JSON for any client

builtOnTitle: Powered by legalize-es. Distributed as a single binary.
builtOnBody: LexFlow is a thin, intelligent layer over legalize-es — an open Git-versioned repository of Spanish legislation in Markdown. The final deliverable is a standalone binary per OS: no Docker, no Python install, just download and run.

builtOnTerminal.text: # Clone, sync the corpus, run the server

stackSub: No experimental frameworks, no proprietary cloud — every piece is open source, well-documented and easy to replace.
roadmapTitle: A public roadmap, phase by phase.
roadmapSub: LexFlow is being built in the open. The backend is feature-complete; the React frontend is where the next milestones land.

roadmap.name: Foundation
roadmap.desc: Repo scaffolding, CI/CD, branch protection, dependabot.

roadmap.name: Core API
roadmap.desc: legalize-es parser, REST endpoints, full-text search.

roadmap.name: Knowledge graph
roadmap.desc: NetworkX model, PageRank, clustering, navigation endpoints.

roadmap.name: Legal chat
roadmap.desc: Five LLM providers, FastMCP tool server, conversation history.

roadmap.name: Dashboards
roadmap.desc: Compliance + analytics Plotly panels with interactive filters.

roadmap.name: React frontend
roadmap.desc: Layout, law explorer, graph canvas, chat UI, dashboards.

authors.title: The people who build it.
authors.sub: LexFlow is the open-source work of two people. If the direction of the project interests you, or you'd like to contribute, their GitHub profile is where the code lives.

list.name: Víctor
list.bio: Owns the architecture, maintains the repository, writes most of the FastAPI backend and React frontend, and coordinates release notes.

list.name: Santiago
list.bio: Co-maintainer on the project. Assigned to every active epic, contributes PR reviews and strengthens the graph and chat code.

ctaTitle: Spin it up in under a minute.
ctaSub: Clone the repo, install with uv, and the full API is live at localhost:8000 with interactive docs. Star the project to follow along.

footer.tagline: Spanish legislation, alive and navigable. An open-source platform built on legalize-es.
footer.legal: Apache 2.0 · pre-alpha · Built with care in 2026
